content.problemStatement:
Read problem statements in RussianIn a game, you can choose when to enter and exit, but you can only do this exactly once. Choosing to enter and exit the game at time lll and rrr respectively will cost you k‚ãÖ(r‚àíl)k\cdot (r-l)k‚ãÖ(r‚àíl) coins.

The game has nnn items. Item iii is valued at viv_ivi‚Äã coins. You can only collect item viv_ivi‚Äã if you are in the game during the whole time period of [xi,yi][x_i,y_i][xi‚Äã,yi‚Äã]; that is, l‚â§xi‚â§yi‚â§rl \le x_i \le y_i \le rl‚â§xi‚Äã‚â§yi‚Äã‚â§r. Your profit after the game is the total value of the items collected minus the cost of entering and exiting the game.

You want to enter and exit the game so that your profit is maximized. Find that maximized profit.

###Input:


The first line contains two integers, nnn and kkk.
Then, nnn lines follow. The iii-th line of these contains three integers, xi,yi,vix_i,y_i,v_ixi‚Äã,yi‚Äã,vi‚Äã.
###Output:
Print a single line containing the maximized profit.

###Constraints


1‚â§n‚â§1051\le n\le 10^51‚â§n‚â§105
1‚â§vi,k‚â§1091 \le v_i ,k\le 10^91‚â§vi‚Äã,k‚â§109
‚àí109‚â§xi,yi‚â§109-10^9 \le x_i,y_i\le 10^9‚àí109‚â§xi‚Äã,yi‚Äã‚â§109
xi‚â§yix_i \le y_ixi‚Äã‚â§yi‚Äã
###Subtasks:
**Subtask #1 (15 points):** n‚â§20n \le 20n‚â§20

**Subtask #2 (25 points):** n‚â§1000n \le 1000n‚â§1000

Explanation:
